PUH-BrianMartell Theorem 59 (Riemann Zeta Zeros as Rebound Symmetry Points, Critical Line as Rebound Axis, Primes as Stable E₈ Knots) The Riemann zeta function ζ(s) = ∑ 1/n^s encodes the Rebound event in number-theoretic space. Its non-trivial zeros lie on the critical line Re(s) = 1/2 because that line is the mathematical image of the rebound axis where matter and antimatter universes expand symmetrically. The spiral convergence to the origin is the Super Planck Star collapse. The primes are the irreducible stable knots in the E₈ lattice — the topological atoms that survive folding and dictate the distribution of all stable particles and structures. Proving the Riemann Hypothesis in PUH follows directly from E₈ geometry: all meaningful events occur on the symmetry axis of the rebound cone.
